stories: Good morning Sean, it's Thursday, the twenty-eighth of May
They only become female when the dominant female in the group dies — which means Nemo's dad would technically have become Nemo's mum
You have a stay at the Westin Desaru Coast Resort in Bandar Penawar through to Monday the first of June
GastroBeats — the food and music festival — is making a comeback in June
And the Seng Choon egg farm is set to relocate after Lim Chu Kang land-use changes were announced, with four other farms also affected as Singapore reconfigures that agricultural corridor
Globally, the dominant story is the US-Israeli war on Iran, now three months in
Ceasefire talks are inching forward, but the White House flatly denied an Iranian state TV report claiming a draft agreement was in place, calling it a complete fabrication
Markets are reacting unevenly: Japan's Nikkei briefly topped sixty-five thousand for the first time on Hormuz reopening hopes; South Korea's Kospi also hit a record high
Good morning Sean, it's Thursday the twenty-eighth of May, 2026
You've got a long weekend kicking off today with a stay at the Westin Desaru — well earned
Fun fact: May is the only month that cannot be abbreviated
The Anglo-Saxons called it the Month of Three Milkings because spring grass made cows so productive they needed milking three times daily
Singapore's Q1 GDP has been revised sharply higher to six percent, up from 4
6 in the advance estimate, driven by financial services and manufacturing
April inflation came in below forecast at 1
8 percent versus an expected two percent, with core at 1
